limitations: Operator chat timing inside this slice is approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
The 0.6.23 package report proves build inventory only; it records distributed:false and does not establish live prerelease authority.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator corrected the lane explicitly: current testing is on the core player, not the `modern_tv` hosted proof, so recovery work must stay on the direct-player playlist and load path instead of drifting back into hosted witness reasoning.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Repo review isolated the wrong policy seam: `0.6.19` changed direct file selection from sibling-playlist recovery to single-item behavior, which is the wrong rule for the operator's core-player workflow and should have been rolled back with the rest of the drift-period compiled behavior.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.23 cleanly after restoring the pre-0.6.19 sibling-playlist expansion behavior in the shared player media resolver.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Regenerated the 0.6.23 package inventory with -SkipVarDistribute and confirmed it remains build-only with distributed:false.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Deployed the raw direct core-player asset and plugin as the live 0.6.23 alpha dev_deploy seam, keeping hosted proof out of live authority for this recovery slice.

decisions: 0.6.23 is its own semantic version because it reverts an operator-visible playlist behavior policy after 0.6.22 had already been built for testing.
The live authority seam for this slice is the raw direct core-player dev_deploy asset plus plugin, not the hosted modern_tv witness seam and not the .var package.
A compiled behavior-policy change made during a high-drift period must be treated as rollback-candidate code during backoff, not as harmless context.
openQuestions: Does 0.6.23 restore truthful Next, scrub, and movie playback behavior for the operator's core-player manual-attach workflow?
After this restore, are there any remaining playlist seams that fail without bringing hosted proof or preset behavior back into the core-player lane?
notes: This slice intentionally stays off the hosted modern_tv proof path except as historical witness context.
The 0.6.23 package report exists only as build inventory and does not compete with the raw direct core-player dev_deploy authority.
